channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: British couple rescued from Spain wildfire ravine after severe burns
item.description: A British couple has been rescued from a ravine in Spain after suffering severe burns during wildfires that devastated the Almeria province. The pair, found semi-conscious and critically injured, were...

item.title: YouTuber charged with trespassing after filming fugitive cop killer's hideout
item.description: A 43-year-old man has been charged with trespassing after filming the secret hideout of Dezi Freeman, a fugitive who killed two police officers in 2025. The incident occurred at a property on Murray R...

item.title: Tanzania's Government and Private Sector Collaborate to Boost Investment Through Vision 2050
item.description: Tanzania continues to attract local and international investors due to its commitment to peace and stability, with government officials emphasizing the importance of creating a business-friendly envir...

item.title: Private Melbourne development offers affordable rentals without public subsidy
item.description: A new privately funded build-to-rent project in Melbourne's eastern suburbs, developed by Local, has introduced 33 affordable rental units at below-market rates without relying on public subsidies. Th...

item.title: Sen. Legarda Advocates for Protecting Fishers' Maritime Rights in West Philippine Sea
item.description: As the Philippines commemorates the 10th anniversary of the 2016 arbitral ruling on the South China Sea, Senator Loren Legarda reiterated the nation's commitment to upholding maritime rights through i...

item.title: A youth pastor reflects on 20 years since his wife's tragic death in Utah
item.description: The article recounts the story of a youth pastor who is revisiting the tragic event of 20 years ago when his wife fell to her death in Utah. It explores the emotional journey of the pastor as he grapp...

item.title: Manqoba Mngqithi's transfer to Kaizer Chiefs blocked by external interference
item.description: Manqoba Mngqithi, the Tanzanian champions' head coach, has confirmed that his potential move to Kaizer Chiefs was blocked by external interference before negotiations could begin. Despite genuine inte...

item.title: Tips for Engaging YouTube Comments
item.description: The article explores strategies for creating YouTube comments that boost engagement. It highlights the importance of authenticity, humor, and relevance in comments to spark conversations and attract l...

item.title: Chef Bal Shares Samosa Kale Chaat Recipe for a Unique Twist on Traditional Snacks
item.description: This article presents a detailed recipe for Samosa Kale Chaat, a fusion dish combining Indian samosa with a vibrant kale chaat. The recipe includes step-by-step instructions for preparing the dough, f...

item.title: Dyonne Rachael Bromstad and Her Siblings: A Family Profile
item.description: The article explores the life of Dyonne Rachael Bromstad, an American interior designer, and her relationship with her famous sibling, David Reed Bromstad. David, known for winning HGTV's Design Star ...

item.title: APC Secures All Seats in Edo LG Election
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) has won all 18 chairmanship seats and 192 councillorship positions in Edo State's local government elections. Edo State Independent Electoral Commission (EDSIEC) ch...

item.title: India Honors Late Qatar Leader Sheikh Hamad with National Mourning
item.description: The Indian government has declared July 13 as a day of national mourning to honor Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, the late ruler of Qatar. Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed condolences, callin...

item.title: Government Updates on Migration Management and Policy Interventions

item.title: Investments in Health and Nutrition Boost Economic Growth in Central West Africa
item.description: The article highlights how investments in health, nutrition, and early childhood development are critical for unlocking economic growth and social transformation in Central and West Africa. Experts em...

item.title: UN special rapporteurs question India's electoral roll revision over alleged impact on minorities
item.description: Three United Nations special rapporteurs have written to the Government of India expressing concerns about the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls, stating that the exercise may have a...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte Begins
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte has officially begun, marking a historic moment in Philippine politics. Four key articles of impeachment are being considered: alleged misuse of P6...

item.title: Jim Acosta Analyzes Poll Showing Dr. Annie Andrews Closing In On Lindsey Graham Amid His Death
item.description: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) died Saturday night from a sudden and unexpected illness, with his office citing a 'brief and sudden illness' as the cause. Hours before his medical incident, a vira...

item.title: Pakistan Urges Restraint Amid Middle East Escalation to Promote Regional Peace
item.description: Pakistan has expressed 'deep concern' over the escalating tensions in the Middle East following recent attacks by the US and Iran, with Gulf countries also facing strikes. The Foreign Office reiterate...

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu Face Criticism for Leveraging Lindsey Graham's Death to Advance Political Agendas
item.description: The article details the backlash against U.S. President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for capitalizing on the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) to push their p...

item.title: Bangladesh Faces Severe Monsoon Impact: 50 Lives Lost, Thousands Displaced
item.description: Bangladesh is grappling with devastating monsoon floods and landslides that have claimed at least 50 lives and displaced tens of thousands of people. The crisis has hit the southeastern districts hard...
